title: Crispy Spicy Canadian Lamb Roast
description: A lean lamb leg slowcooked to tender perfection, finished with a spicy rub and a crisp, caramelized crust that delivers a Canadian flavor kick.

ingredients.name: Lean lamb leg
ingredients.notes: about 2 lb, trimmed of excess fat

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together salt, pepper, cayenne, smoked paprika, cumin, rosemary, and minced garlic to form a spice rub.
Pat the lamb leg dry with paper towels, then rub the spice mixture all over the meat.
Heat olive oil in a large oven‑proof skillet over high heat until shimmering. Sear the lamb leg for 2 minutes on each side until a deep brown crust forms.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven, pour chicken broth around the meat, and cover with foil. Roast for 150 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
Remove the skillet from the oven and increase the temperature to 425°F (220°C). Broil for 5 minutes, watching closely, to crisp the outer surface.
Let the lamb rest for 10 minutes before slicing. This allows juices to redistribute and maintains a juicy interior.

Slice the roast, serve hot, and optionally garnish with a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired. Enjoy the crispy, spicy Canadian lamb with a low‑fat side such as roasted root vegetables or a fresh green salad.
